anchor:
We’re expanding our cloud ops team and looking for a **mid‑level SRE** who can jump in right away.  
The ideal candidate knows **Kubernetes, Terraform, gRPC, and Go** (Python is a plus, but not a must).  
We’re based in Austin, so the role is **on‑site** and involves building and maintaining our multi‑cluster infrastructure, automating deployments, and driving cost‑efficiency while keeping uptime high.  
If you love working in a fast‑paced environment with a mix of infra‑as‑code and real‑time monitoring, let’s chat!

positive.summary: Mid-level Site Reliability Engineer with 4.6 years of experience building and maintaining multi-cluster Kubernetes infrastructures using Terraform and Go.

work_experience.description: Implemented CI/CD pipelines with Terraform and Docker to streamline application deployments. Monitored services with Prometheus and Alertmanager, reducing incident response time by 35%. Contributed to a Go-based microservice that handled real-time transit routing.

Lead the automation of multi-cluster Kubernetes deployments across three regions using Terraform. Built and maintained gRPC-based service discovery in Go, achieving 99.9% uptime and reducing infra costs by 22% through efficient resource provisioning.

projects.name: ClusterCost
projects.description: Developed a cost-metric dashboard that aggregates Kubernetes cluster usage from AWS CloudWatch, providing actionable insights for cost optimization.

projects.name: GRPC-Auth
projects.description: Built a lightweight Go-based gRPC service for token-based authentication used across microservices in the care platform.

projects.name: Infra-Blueprint
projects.description: Designed reusable Terraform modules for deploying multi-region Kubernetes clusters, enabling rapid environment spin-up for QA and production.

certifications: Google Cloud Professional Cloud DevOps Engineer

negative.summary: Mid-Level Frontend Engineer with 4 years of experience building reusable UI components for media SaaS, proficient in React, TypeScript, Tailwind, and GraphQL.

work_experience.description: Led development of reusable component library for appointment scheduling platform using React, TypeScript, Tailwind, and GraphQL. Implemented unit and integration tests with Jest and React Testing Library; integrated CI/CD pipelines with GitHub Actions and Docker.

Built media SaaS dashboard for real-time ride data visualization using React, TypeScript, Tailwind, and GraphQL. Focused on component scalability and performance optimization. Maintained CI/CD pipeline with GitHub Actions and Docker.

projects.name: MediaKit
projects.description: Reusable component library for media SaaS built with React and TypeScript, styled with Tailwind and styled-components.

projects.name: RideDashboard
projects.description: Real-time dashboard for Swvl data, built with React, TypeScript, GraphQL, and Tailwind.

projects.name: GraphQL Playground
projects.description: Custom GraphQL client UI for internal use, using React and TypeScript.

anchor:
**Senior Android Engineer – Kotlin 1.8 / Jetpack Compose**  
*Full‑time, On‑site (San Francisco, CA, or equivalent) – start date ASAP*

We’re building a next‑generation mobile platform that integrates real‑time data streaming, advanced AI analytics, and multi‑device workflows. We need a senior Android professional who can architect and ship production‑grade apps that run flawlessly on Android 13+ while staying on the bleeding edge of the ecosystem.

**Key Requirements**

| Spec | Details |
|------|---------|
| **Programming Language** | Kotlin 1.8 (stable release) |
| **Frameworks/Libraries** | Jetpack Compose 1.4, Material3, Navigation Compose, Compose Navigation, Hilt 2.44 DI, Accompanist, Kotlinx Coroutines, Flow |
| **Build Tools** | Gradle 8.0+ with Kotlin DSL; Android SDK Platform 33 (Android 13); Custom build variants for A/B testing |
| **UI/UX** | Expertise in designing responsive, high‑fidelity UIs with Compose layouts; Accessiblity integration (TalkBack, Switch Access) |
| **Testing** | Unit: JUnit 5, MockK; UI: Espresso 3.5, Compose Test 1.4; Continuous integration with Firebase Test Lab |
| **CI/CD** | GitHub Actions, Gradle Wrapper, Fastlane for beta/Play Store distribution |
| **Architecture** | MVVM with Clean Architecture, Repository pattern; Offline persistence with Room 2.6; Remote data via Retrofit 2.9 + Kotlin Flow |
| **Performance** | Proficient in profiling (Android Profiler, Systrace), optimizing large lists (LazyColumn), and memory usage |
| **Collaboration** | DevOps/QA integration, Agile (Scrum/Kanban), Code reviews, pair programming |
| **Security** | Knowledge of GDPR, secure storage (Encrypted SharedPreferences, Android Keystore), API key handling |
| **Soft Skills** | 5+ years of Android dev, strong mentorship, excellent communication, proactive problem‑solving |

**Responsibilities**

1. Lead the architectural direction for the Android app across 3+ product lines.
2. Own the implementation of critical feature sets: real‑time WebSocket integration, adaptive UI for foldable devices, and in-app AI suggestions.
3. Conduct code reviews and mentor at least 2 junior engineers.
4. Define and enforce coding standards, performance benchmarks, and security best practices.
5. Collaborate with cross‑functional teams (Product, Design, Backend, QA) to deliver high‑quality releases on schedule.

**Why Join Us?**

- Work on cutting‑edge Android features with the latest Kotlin and Compose releases.
- Opportunities to influence architectural decisions and optimize the mobile stack.
- Competitive salary, comprehensive benefits, and a culture that values continuous learning.

If you’re passionate about building robust, high‑performance Android experiences and want to make an impact from day one, we’d love to hear from you. Please include a link to your portfolio or significant past projects and any relevant GitHub contributions.

positive.summary: Senior Android Engineer with 7+ years of experience building production‑grade apps on Android 13+ using Kotlin, Jetpack Compose, and modern architecture patterns.

work_experience.description: Built streaming music player and offline caching using Kotlin and Jetpack Compose. Implemented custom A/B testing build variants and integrated Accessibility features across all screens.

Developed order‑processing and real‑time delivery tracking modules using Kotlin and Jetpack Compose. Introduced MVVM Clean Architecture, Room persistence, and Retrofit networking, reducing build times by 25%. Mentored junior developers and enforced coding standards.

Lead the Android architecture for three product lines, implementing real‑time WebSocket communication, foldable‑device adaptive UI, and AI‑driven suggestion features on Android 13+. Managed code quality, performance benchmarks, and security compliance.

projects.name: RealTimeChat
projects.description: Android chat app with real‑time WebSocket communication, adaptive UI for foldable devices, and AI‑powered suggestions.

projects.name: FoldableUI
projects.description: Library providing responsive Compose layouts and utilities for foldable Android devices.

projects.name: AIAnalytics
projects.description: Integrated AI analytics module into existing Android apps using Kotlin Flow and Jetpack Compose.

work_experience.description: Designed and implemented Spark-based data pipelines, fine-tuned BERT models for intent classification, and integrated ML workflows into production.
Lead deployment of recommendation models on Kubernetes, monitored model drift with Prometheus and Grafana, and optimized inference latency.

projects.description: Trained a BERT model on e-commerce reviews, achieving 93% accuracy on a test set.

anchor:
**Senior Frontend Engineer – React/Next.js**  
* 5+ yrs professional experience, ideal for lead/tech‑lead role.  
* Deep knowledge of React (hooks, context, performance optimization) + Next.js 13/SSG/SSR.  
* TypeScript, CSS-in-JS (styled-components) or Tailwind CSS; responsive UI design.  
* Strong Git, unit tests (Jest, React Testing Library), end‑to‑end tests (Cypress).  
* Familiar with GraphQL (Apollo Client), REST, and data fetching strategies.  
* Experience with CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, Azure Pipelines) and containerization (Docker).  
* Comfortable deploying to Next.js hosting (Vercel, Netlify) or container platforms (AWS ECS, GCP Cloud Run).  
* Agile/Scrum, mentoring junior devs, code‑review culture.  
* Bachelor's degree in CS or equivalent.  
* Fully remote in the U.S. or hybrid (NYC, Boston).  
* Full‑time, non‑C2C, start ASAP.  
* Preferred: experience with server‑less functions, S3, CloudFront, or similar cloud storage solutions.

anchor:
**Senior Frontend Engineer – React/Next.js**  
* 5+ yrs professional experience, ideal for lead/tech‑lead role.  
* Deep knowledge of React (hooks, context, performance optimization) + Next.js 13/SSG/SSR.  
* TypeScript, CSS-in-JS (styled-components) or Tailwind CSS; responsive UI design.  
* Strong Git, unit tests (Jest, React Testing Library), end‑to‑end tests (Cypress).  
* Familiar with GraphQL (Apollo Client), REST, and data fetching strategies.  
* Experience with CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, Azure Pipelines) and containerization (Docker).  
* Comfortable deploying to Next.js hosting (Vercel, Netlify) or container platforms (AWS ECS, GCP Cloud Run).  
* Agile/Scrum, mentoring junior devs, code‑review culture.  
* Bachelor's degree in CS or equivalent.  
* Fully remote in the U.S. or hybrid (NYC, Boston).  
* Full‑time, non‑C2C, start ASAP.  
* Preferred: experience with server‑less functions, S3, CloudFront, or similar cloud storage solutions.

anchor:
**Mid‑Level Frontend Engineer (React 18.x, Next.js 13.x, Tailwind CSS 3.3, Chakra UI 5.x)**  

- 2–4 years of proven experience building production‑grade web applications for consumer‑facing SaaS platforms  
- Mastery of the Next.js 13 App Router, server‑component rendering, Image Optimization API, and Edge Middleware (Node 18 runtime)  
- Extensive hands‑on work with Tailwind CSS 3.3 (JIT, responsive breakpoints, custom plugins) and Chakra UI 5.5 (theme overrides, component composition, SSR‑friendly styling)  
- Strong TypeScript proficiency (TS 4.8+), ES6+ JavaScript patterns, and functional‑react design (React 18‑strict mode, useSyncExternalStore, useId)  
- Expertise in performance optimization: code‑splitting, bundle analysis with webpack 5, lazy‑loading of components, dynamic imports, strategic use of SWR for data fetching, and cache‑throttle patterns for image and API calls  
- Familiarity with the new “Incremental Static Regeneration” (ISR) feature of Next.js, and advanced static‑generation strategies for large‑scale product catalogs  
- Proficient in automated UI testing with Vitest + @testing-library/react, snapshot testing, and end‑to‑end tests via Playwright (strictly typed test suites, parallel execution)  
- Comfortable architecting and consuming micro‑frontend modules via Module Federation or isolated React apps (React 18 shallow rendering, shared dependencies)  
- CI/CD experience with GitHub Actions, Docker‑based staging pipelines, and deployment to Vercel with custom domains, SSL, and preview URLs for each PR  
- Solid knowledge of accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA), linting (ESLint, Stylelint), and code quality tooling (Prettier, SonarQube)  
- Excellent collaborative mindset: active participation in daily scrums, code reviews, pair‑programming, and design‑system grooming sessions (Figma ↔︎ Chakra + Tailwind)  
- Fluency in English (written & spoken); ability to work distributed with North American time zones (PST, MST, EST) or a bi‑weekly on‑site presence in SF, Austin, or NYC  
- Open to full‑time or 6–12 month contract roles; relocation to a primary US office is preferred for long‑term engagement  
- Bonus points for experience with Figma‑to‑Code automation (Storybook Docs, Chromatic), Lighthouse 90+ score enforcement, and integrating third‑party analytics (Segment, Snowplow)  

**Start Date:** 30 days from hiring decision or sooner with prior notice.

positive.summary: Mid-level Frontend Engineer with 3.3 years of experience building production-grade SaaS web applications using Next.js, React, Tailwind, and Chakra UI.

work_experience.description: Built and maintained real-time ride booking UI using Next.js 13 App Router with server components, integrated SWR for data fetching, optimized performance through code-splitting and lazy-loading, and leveraged Tailwind CSS 3.3 and Chakra UI 5.5 for responsive design. Deployed with Docker, GitHub Actions CI/CD, and Vercel preview URLs for each PR.

Led frontend team for subscription service platform, implementing Next.js 13 App Router, Edge Middleware on Node 18, ISR for large product catalogs, and micro-frontend architecture via Module Federation. Automated UI tests with Vitest, @testing-library/react, and Playwright, achieved Lighthouse 90+ score enforcement, and integrated Segment and Snowplow analytics. Designed Figma-to-code automation for Chakra UI and Tailwind components, maintaining WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility.

Developed and optimized e-commerce front for subscription-based model using Next.js 13 App Router and React 18, incorporating Tailwind CSS 3.3 and Chakra UI 5.5. Implemented performance tuning with webpack 5, dynamic imports, and strategic cache-throttle patterns with SWR. Built CI/CD pipeline with GitHub Actions, Dockerized staging environment, and deployed to Vercel with custom domain and SSL. Conducted automated UI testing with Vitest and Playwright, ensuring 95% code coverage. Maintained accessibility compliance (WCAG 2.1 AA).

anchor: Seeking an **Senior Blockchain Engineering Lead** (5+ years, Solidity/Ethereum) to architect and deliver enterprise‑grade smart contract solutions for Layer‑2 ecosystems (Polygon, Arbitrum, zk‑Rollups). This role involves leading a cross‑functional team, driving code‑review standards, and partnering with product and security stakeholders to define long‑term Web3 strategy. Candidates must demonstrate mastery of advanced Solidity security patterns, experience with cross‑chain bridges, and proficiency in Hardhat, Foundry, and automated verification tools. Remote or U.S. (NYC/SF) preferred; full‑time, direct hire. If you are a seasoned blockchain visionary with a portfolio of audited contracts, contact us for a confidential discussion.

negative.summary: Entry-level embedded firmware engineer with 1.3 years of experience in STM32 development, FreeRTOS 10.4.x, and low-power design on STM32H7/H5 platforms.

work_experience.description: Implemented current‑sense ADC driver for DC‑DC converter monitoring on STM32H750 dual‑core board. Leveraged HAL ADC with DMA, interrupt routines, and FreeRTOS queue integration. Applied MISRA‑C:2012 guidelines, designed Unity unit tests, and used Segger J‑Link for debugging. Maintained GitHub repository with GitLab CI for static analysis and automated flashing using STM32CubeProgrammer.

Developed low‑power UART and DMA drivers for the order collection device, utilizing STM32Cube HAL, CMSIS, FreeRTOS 10.4.x, and ensuring <64‑byte stack per task. Configured STM32H743 board with STM32CubeMX, implemented tickless idle and power‑management features, validated code quality with cppcheck and Unity unit tests, and managed continuous integration via GitLab CI. Debugged with Segger J-Link and flashed with STM32CubeProgrammer.

projects.description: UART driver for STM32H743 with FreeRTOS integration, optimized for <64‑byte stack per task, includes interrupt and DMA handling.
